title: Why Ancient History Matters
body:
Ancient history matters because so many core human institutions were first formalised there. To understand law, empire, religion, and statecraft, you need to understand their earliest large-scale forms.

It also matters because it gives perspective on the present. Civilisations rise and fall, technologies change, and power shifts; ancient history makes that obvious.

The field builds intellectual humility. You learn to work with incomplete evidence, resist overconfidence, and tell the difference between what is known and what is merely plausible.

Those habits are useful far beyond history itself.
keyPoints: Ancient history reveals the roots of institutions
It normalises change, fragility, and transition
It helps people compare civilisations without myth-making
It gives context to religion, law, and politics
furtherReading: The Silk Roads by Peter Frankopan
The Ancient City by Joseph Rykwert
